summary: The Docker-based reproduction confirms that html2pdf.js executes attacker-controlled JavaScript through the incomplete innerHTML sanitization when processing HTML strings.
expected_security_impact: Running the reproduction should cause the crafted HTML payload to trigger JavaScript execution through an event handler (onerror) that was not removed by the script-tag-only sanitization.
actual_observed_result: Running ./reproduction/run.sh built and launched a headless browser environment, loaded html2pdf.js with the crafted XSS payload, and confirmed JavaScript execution through console output showing the attacker-controlled proof string.
verification_method: The reproduction uses a headless browser to load html2pdf.js and execute the XSS payload. Success is verified by checking browser console output for the attacker-controlled proof string, demonstrating that JavaScript executed in the page context.
evidence_produced_by_run: Browser console contains the attacker-controlled proof string 'XSS_PROOF_EVOHUNT_POC_SUCCESS'
The XSS payload executed despite script-tag removal, proving incomplete sanitization
No error conditions or exceptions occurred during normal html2pdf processing

reproduction.source_acquisition: run.sh clones the target repository on the host, checks out the pinned vulnerable commit, and mounts that checkout into the container at /target-repo.
